articles.description: Mercedes' George Russell wins the sprint race at the Chinese Grand Prix after a frantic scrap in the early laps with Lewis Hamilton's Ferrari.
articles.title: Chinese GP sprint 2026: Russell wins eventful race

articles.description: Scotland spent decades staring in the window as Ireland and Wales partied in the Six Nations. In Dublin on Saturday, they finally have a shot at glory.
articles.title: Six Nations 2026: Ireland v Scotland preview

articles.description: While Arsenal continue their challenge on four fronts, their style of play continues to be criticised by some. Is it justified?
articles.title: Would Arsenal be the 'ugliest Premier League title winners ever'?

articles.description: Rory McIlroy hopes a birdie on the last hole of his second round will be enough to mean he makes the cut at the Players Championship.
articles.title: Players Championship: Rory McIlroy on cut mark as Xander Schauffele sets pace

articles.description: England bowler Mark Wood says his injured knee is improving but admits he has started thinking about life after cricket for the first time.
articles.title: Mark Wood: Injured England bowler 'improving' but won't push recovery

articles.description: The Bahrain and Saudi Arabia Grands Prix scheduled for next month are set to be cancelled as a result of the Middle East war.
articles.title: Bahrain and Saudi Arabia GPs to be cancelled amid Middle East war

articles.description: Premier League champions, FA Cup winners and club legends have all suffered the unwanted fate, but which relegated side really was "too good to go down"?
articles.title: Too good to go down? Ranking shock Premier League relegations

articles.description: The average starting price of the 28 winners hit a 10-year high as 2026 marked a difficult year for punters at the Cheltenham Festival.
articles.title: Cheltenham Festival 2026: Was it the year of the outsider at the four-day festival?

articles.description: Ireland captain Caelan Doris says there is no "second chance" as his side prepare to host Scotland in Saturday's Six Nations Triple Crown decider.
articles.title: Six Nations 2026: 'No second chance' as Ireland gear up for Triple Crown decider against Scotland

articles.description: Tottenham interim boss Igor Tudor says his players can "cry or fight" as they seek to avoid relegation from the Premier League.
articles.title: Igor Tudor: Tottenham interim boss says players can 'cry or fight' amid dire situation
